rows.modal: must / mustn't, have to, need to
rows.example: Students **must** bring their ID cards to the exam. / You **have to / need to** register before you can log in.

rows.modal: don't have to, don't need to, needn't
rows.example: You **don't have to / don't need to / needn't** print the ticket — they'll scan it on your phone.
We **had to** walk home because we missed the last bus.

rows.modal: didn't have to, didn't need to
rows.example: There was food at the hotel, so we **didn't have to / didn't need to** go to a restaurant.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: In everyday speech, **have to** is used more often than **must**. **Must** tends to appear in signs, rules and written instructions.
items: "I **have to** finish this before tomorrow," he said.
Passengers **must** keep their bags with them at all times.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: **Mustn't** and **don't have to** carry very different meanings.

notes.correct: You **mustn't** open that door! (= It is forbidden — do not do it.)
You **don't have to** open that door. (= You have a choice — it is not necessary.)

rows.example: She's been working since dawn — she **must** be exhausted. / That **can't / couldn't** be right — I checked the answer twice.
The parcel **should / ought to** arrive by Thursday — it was sent two days ago.
I'm not sure where she is — she **could / may / might** be at the library.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We typically use **must**, **can't** and **couldn't** when we have evidence for our conclusion.
items: His coat is here but he isn't. He **must** have stepped outside for a moment.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: To speculate about past events, use a modal followed by the perfect infinitive (have + past participle).

sections.title: A: Complete with must, have to, need to or mustn't
sections.instructions: Write the best modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... wear a helmet when you ride a motorbike.
We ...... leave now or we'll miss the train.
I ...... call Mia tonight. It's her birthday.
Visitors ...... feed the animals at the zoo.
Students ...... bring a pen for the exam.
You ...... touch that wire. It's dangerous.
I ...... finish this report before five.
Do we ...... book the tickets online?

sections.title: B: Choose don't have to, don't need to or needn't
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with a form showing no obligation.

items.sentence: You ...... bring any food. We'll order pizza.
It's Sunday tomorrow, so I ...... get up early.
You ...... wait for me — I can walk home.
We ...... print the tickets; they're on my phone.
Ethan ...... wear a uniform at his new school.
You ...... hurry. We've got lots of time.
I ...... cook tonight because Mum already has.
You ...... call me back now — text me later.

sections.title: C: Past obligation
sections.instructions: Complete using had to, didn't have to or didn't need to.

items.sentence: We ...... take a taxi because there were no buses.
I ...... buy milk because there was some at home.
Nora ...... stay late at work yesterday.
They ...... come to school on Monday because it was a holiday.
I ...... use a dictionary; I knew all the words.
Sam ...... wear glasses for a few months after the operation.
We ...... pay for the meal. It was free.
Marta ...... work last Saturday, so she visited her grandparents.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct phrase
sections.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: You **mustn't / don't have to** park here if you don't want to.
You **mustn't / don't have to** smoke in the hospital.
We **had to / didn't have to** queue for tickets because we booked online.
I **need to / mustn't** remember to send that email.
Everyone **must / needn't** show ID at the entrance.
Leo **had to / didn't have to** walk home because he missed the last bus.
You **needn't / mustn't** shout — I can hear you.
We **don't have to / mustn't** forget our passports.

sections.title: E: Probability and possibility
sections.instructions: Complete with must, can't, couldn't, should, ought to, could, may or might.

items.sentence: That ...... be Ella. She said she'd be here at seven.
This ...... be Ben's jacket — it's much too small for him.
They ...... arrive late if traffic is bad.
You ...... hear from the college in a few days.
I'm not sure where Jo is. She ...... be at the gym.
The lights are off and nobody's answering. They ...... be at home.
The weather looks fine, so the match ...... start on time.
Take an umbrella — it ...... rain later.
You look tired. You ...... go to bed earlier.
This ...... be Olivia's phone. She has exactly the same case.
We ...... pay now; the meal is included in the ticket.
You ...... run in the corridor. It's dangerous.
He left home at six, so he ...... be here soon.
She ...... finish the project yesterday, so she stayed at the office late.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one modal error. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: You don't have to use your phone while driving.
I must to call Grandma tonight.
We had to not wear a uniform at my old school.
That must be James; I just saw him in class.
They needn't to bring their books tomorrow.
This might is the right answer, but I'm not sure.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
It's necessary for all visitors to sign in. **MUST**
All visitors ...... in.

It wasn't necessary for us to pay. **HAVE**
We ...... to pay.

I'm almost sure that's Leo's bike. **MUST**
That ...... Leo's bike.

Maybe we'll go to the coast this weekend. **MIGHT**
We ...... to the coast this weekend.

It's prohibited to swim here. **MUSTN'T**
You ...... here.

It's probable that they'll arrive by noon. **SHOULD**
They ...... by noon.

sections.title: I: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Write one suitable modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... bring your own laptop; we have enough at school.
The museum closes at five, so we ...... leave now.
It's very cold and his coat is still here. He ...... be outside.
I ...... finish this tonight, but I'll do it tomorrow morning.
She studied hard, so she ...... pass the exam.
You ...... copy in exams. It's against the rules.
The lights are on, so they ...... be at home.
We ...... walk to school yesterday because Dad drove us.
I'm not certain, but this message ...... be from our teacher.
I forgot my key, so I ...... wait outside for an hour.

sections.title: J: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
It's not necessary to arrive early.
You ...... arrive early.

You are not allowed to use your phone.
You ...... your phone.

I'm sure she is at home.
She ...... at home.

It was obligatory to wear a uniform.
We ...... a uniform.

It's possible that he missed the bus.
He ...... the bus.

I don't think it's necessary to book.
You ...... book.
